PLC 2 – QUICKSTART GUIDE
PLC 2: THE TRANSFORMATION
Overview
Your second piece of Prelaunch Content is all about the “what” – what is the
transformation and how is it going to change your prospects’ lives? PLC 2 generally has
more teaching than PLC 1. You want to teach some type of tip or strategy that is truly
valuable.
The Framework
Here’s the general recipe for a strong PLC 2:
1. 	Thanks and recap: Thank people for their comments and questions from
PLC 1, and then give a quick recap of PLC 1.
2. 	Recap the opportunity: You won’t spend as much time as you did in PLC 1,
but you need to quickly recap the opportunity. Don’t ever assume that your
prospect has seen or paid attention to or remembered PLC 1. Remember, they’ve
got busy lives and your launch isn’t nearly as important to them as it is to you.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 5 --

ProductLaunchFormula.com 	© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
PLC 2 – QUICKSTART GUIDE
3. 	Recap your positioning: You need to remind them who you are and why they
should listen to you. But don’t take too long with this – do it quickly.
4. 	Present a Case Study or do some real teaching: You need to deliver some
real value for your viewer. Teach them one (or more) cool things that they can put
to use quickly.
5. 	Objection crushing: Talk about the top two or three objections and answer
them. You want to go after your prospects’ big objections to the transformation
that you’re promising.
6. 	Foreshadow PLC 3: You need to let them know you have another video coming
soon. Build some anticipation for it by telling them a little about what you’re going
to teach in that video.
7. 	Call to action: Ask for a comment on your launch blog or in social media.
Example
§ 	“Thanks for all the comments. It’s awesome to know there are so many kind
hearted souls...”
§ 	“In the last video I showed you…” (quick)
§ 	“And how I discovered the answer…” (quick)
§ 	“And what’s possible…” (quick)
§ 	“Now I want to take you deeper into this area…” (teach)
§ 	“A lot of people have been asking about…” (crush objections)
§ 	“In the next video…”
§ 	“Please leave a comment letting me know what you think…”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 5 --

ProductLaunchFormula.com 	© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
PLC 2 – QUICKSTART GUIDE
TIPS for powerful content
The following tips can make your Prelaunch Content more effective and powerful:
§ 	Video length: Try to keep the length of your video to 12-18 minutes. The length
will vary depending on your experience and content, but a good rule of thumb is
that your video should be long enough to cover all key elements - and not a minute
longer. As always, if you’re more advanced or experienced, then your videos can be
longer. But be careful not to be boring.
§ 	Screen capture vs. full motion video: Use full motion video when possible
for the following reasons:
o 	Your face on camera creates a more powerful connection.
o 	You being in the video makes you come across as real and relatable.
o 	Talking to the camera vs. reading from a script presents a more natural and
informal (rather than overly professional) tone and can make you more
likable.
o 	However, the most important thing is that you’re comfortable. If you are a
lot more comfortable using screen capture video, then go ahead and do so.
§ 	Website navigation: Include a link for your previous Prelaunch Content on the
webpage so people that missed it can go back and view it (but avoid any other links
or distractions).
§ 	Bonus content: You can add… a PDF, worksheet, checklist, mind map or other
valuable piece of downloadable material to enhance/reinforce/supplement what
you’re teaching.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

ProductLaunchFormula.com 	© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
PLC 2 – QUICKSTART GUIDE
PLC 2 CHECKLIST
¨ Did you continue your story arc or theme from PLC 1?
¨ Did you put your Prelaunch Content behind an opt-in page? (Test your opt-in
process carefully.)
¨ Did you create a hook for PLC 2 that makes people want to watch the video?
¨ Did you recap the first video and launch story?
¨ Did you thank people for their engagement and point out relative responses and
comments from the first video?
¨ Did you show them the “what” either with an overview of the subject or dive deep
into one particular area of the “what”?
¨ Did you present at least one case study or story that teaches valuable information
and continues to paint the opportunity and transformation that will happen for
people?
¨ Did you give out a cool strategy or secret or two? (Remember they don’t have to be
expert level secrets, most people are beginners).
¨ Did you answer the top two or three objections to the change that you’re promising
through stories and examples?
¨ Did you tease PLC 3 with a powerful promise or a partially told intriguing story
that people will want to come back to hear?
¨ Did you include a compelling call to action (call to comment)?
¨ Did you review your video for video/audio quality? (Test your video playback on
different browsers.)
¨ Did you review your delivery? (Do you sound authentic, are you relaxed, is the
energy engaging and appropriate?)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
